nodes.context: Criminal gang controlling labor slugging and strikebreaking in New York's Lower East Side in the early 1900s.
Labor slugging gang allied with Rosenzweig during the Labor Slugger Wars.
Rival labor slugging gang that warred with Rosenzweig in 1913-1914.
Gang led by ex-lieutenant of Rosenzweig, who warned Rosenzweig against reforming his gang.

edges.context: Rosenzweig was an ally of Benny Fein during the Labor Slugger Wars.
Philip Paul began a war with Rosenzweig in 1913, ending with Paul's death in 1914.
Waxey Gordon was an ex-lieutenant of Rosenzweig and later led his own gang.
